468.explanation: This word describes something that moves or happens at a low speed. It is the opposite of fast.

examples.dutch: De slak beweegt langzaam.
examples.english: The snail moves slowly.

examples.dutch: Spreek alsjeblieft langzaam, dan begrijp ik het beter.
examples.english: Please speak slowly, then I understand it better.

469.explanation: This adjective describes someone or something connected to a religion or religious beliefs.

examples.dutch: Mijn oma is erg religieus en gaat elke zondag naar de kerk.
examples.english: My grandma is very religious and goes to church every Sunday.

examples.dutch: Hij leest vaak religieuze boeken.
examples.english: He often reads religious books.

470.explanation: This word describes something that is exciting, causes suspense, or is thrilling. You feel excited or a little nervous about it.

examples.dutch: De film was heel spannend tot het einde.
examples.english: The movie was very thrilling until the end.

examples.dutch: Het is een spannende wedstrijd vandaag.
examples.english: It's an exciting match today.

471.explanation: This adjective describes something that has a circular shape or is shaped like a sphere. It can also mean 'around'.

examples.dutch: De tafel is rond van vorm.
examples.english: The table is round in shape.

examples.dutch: We lopen rond het park.
examples.english: We walk around the park.

472.explanation: This adjective describes something that relates to men or boys, or has characteristics traditionally associated with males. In grammar, it refers to a masculine gender.

examples.dutch: Een leeuw is een mannelijk dier.
examples.english: A lion is a male animal.

examples.dutch: In het Nederlands is 'de man' een mannelijk woord.
examples.english: In Dutch, 'de man' is a masculine word.

473.explanation: This adjective describes something that relates to women or girls, or has characteristics traditionally associated with females. In grammar, it refers to a feminine gender.

examples.dutch: Mijn zus heeft een heel vrouwelijke stijl.
examples.english: My sister has a very feminine style.

examples.dutch: 'De vrouw' is een vrouwelijk woord in het Nederlands.
examples.english: 'De vrouw' is a feminine word in Dutch.

474.explanation: This word describes something that is not easy to do, understand, or deal with. It requires effort or skill.

examples.dutch: Deze som is moeilijk voor mij.
examples.english: This sum is difficult for me.

examples.dutch: Het examen was erg moeilijk.
examples.english: The exam was very difficult.

475.explanation: In grammar, this adjective refers to a grammatical gender that is neither masculine nor feminine. In Dutch, 'het' words are neuter.

examples.dutch: 'Het huis' is een onzijdig woord in het Nederlands.
examples.english: 'Het huis' is a neuter word in Dutch.

examples.dutch: Dit zelfstandig naamwoord is onzijdig.
examples.english: This noun is neuter.

476.explanation: This adjective is used to describe food that has been cooked sufficiently and is ready to eat. It means 'cooked through' or 'done'.

examples.dutch: Is de kip al gaar?
examples.english: Is the chicken done yet?

examples.dutch: De groenten zijn bijna gaar.
examples.english: The vegetables are almost done.

477.explanation: This adverb indicates that something is very close to happening or being true. It means 'almost' or 'nearly'.

examples.dutch: Het is al bijna zes uur.
examples.english: It's almost six o'clock already.

examples.dutch: Ik ben bijna klaar met mijn werk.
examples.english: I am almost finished with my work.

478.explanation: This adverb refers to the initial action or item in a sequence. It means 'first' or 'at first'.

examples.dutch: Eerst gaan we eten, dan naar de film.
examples.english: First we go eat, then to the movie.

examples.dutch: Wie was de eerste die aankwam?
examples.english: Who was the first to arrive?

479.explanation: This adverb means 'at all times' or 'on every occasion'. It describes something that never changes or constantly happens.

examples.dutch: Ik drink altijd koffie in de ochtend.
examples.english: I always drink coffee in the morning.

examples.dutch: Hij is altijd vrolijk.
examples.english: He is always cheerful.

480.explanation: This adverb refers to something happening at the same time as something else. It means 'meanwhile' or 'in the meantime'.

examples.dutch: Zij kookt het avondeten; ondertussen lees ik de krant.
examples.english: She cooks dinner; meanwhile I read the newspaper.

examples.dutch: De bus komt over vijf minuten, ondertussen wachten we hier.
examples.english: The bus arrives in five minutes, meanwhile we wait here.

481.explanation: This adverb indicates a possibility or uncertainty. It means 'maybe' or 'perhaps'.

examples.dutch: Misschien regent het morgen.
examples.english: Maybe it will rain tomorrow.

examples.dutch: Misschien kunnen we samen wandelen.
examples.english: Maybe we can walk together.

482.explanation: This word has several meanings. It can mean 'just' or 'only' when referring to time or quantity, or 'not until'.

examples.dutch: Ik woon hier pas een maand.
examples.english: I have only lived here for a month.

examples.dutch: De film begint pas om negen uur.
examples.english: The movie doesn't start until nine o'clock.

483.explanation: This preposition indicates opposition, contact, or direction towards something. It can mean 'against', 'towards', or 'by'.

examples.dutch: De ladder staat tegen de muur.
examples.english: The ladder is against the wall.

examples.dutch: Ik heb niets tegen jouw idee.
examples.english: I have nothing against your idea.

484.explanation: This verb means to arrange or plan an event or activity. It involves putting things in order.

examples.dutch: Wij willen een groot feest organiseren.
examples.english: We want to organize a big party.

examples.dutch: Zij kan goed reizen organiseren.
examples.english: She is good at organizing trips.

485.explanation: This verb means to be made up of or composed of certain parts. It indicates what something includes or contains.

examples.dutch: De groep bestaat uit drie jongens en twee meisjes.
examples.english: The group consists of three boys and two girls.

examples.dutch: Mijn ontbijt bestaat uit brood en fruit.
examples.english: My breakfast consists of bread and fruit.

486.explanation: This verb means to provide assistance or support to someone. You make it easier for someone to do something.

examples.dutch: Kun je me helpen met de boodschappen?
examples.english: Can you help me with the groceries?

examples.dutch: Ik help graag mijn vrienden.
examples.english: I like to help my friends.

487.explanation: This verb is used specifically to express that you like a particular food or drink. It means 'to like' food or drink.

examples.dutch: Ik lust graag spaghetti.
examples.english: I like spaghetti.

examples.dutch: Lust je een kopje thee?
examples.english: Do you like a cup of tea? / Would you like a cup of tea?

488.explanation: This verb means to divide something into pieces, typically using a sharp tool like a knife. It's commonly used when preparing food.

examples.dutch: Ik snijd de groenten voor het avondeten.
examples.english: I cut the vegetables for dinner.

examples.dutch: Kun je een plak brood snijden?
examples.english: Can you cut a slice of bread?

489.explanation: To 'zetten' means to put or place something, often implying placing it upright or in a specific, deliberate position. It's different from 'leggen' (to lay).

examples.dutch: Ik zet de kopjes op tafel.
examples.english: I put the cups on the table.

examples.dutch: Zij zet de stoel bij het raam.
examples.english: She puts the chair by the window.

490.explanation: To 'wachten' means to stay in one place or delay action until something else happens or someone arrives. You often 'wait for' something or someone.

examples.dutch: Ik wacht op de bus bij de halte.
examples.english: I wait for the bus at the stop.

examples.dutch: Kun je even wachten op mij?
examples.english: Can you wait for me for a moment?

491.explanation: This verb means to go and get something or someone and bring it or them back. It implies movement to retrieve.

examples.dutch: Ik moet mijn jas uit de gang halen.
examples.english: I need to pick up my coat from the hallway.

examples.dutch: Kun je brood halen bij de bakker?
examples.english: Can you pick up bread at the bakery?

492.explanation: To 'pakken' means to take hold of something quickly or firmly with your hand. It can also simply mean to take something.

examples.dutch: Ik pak de bal die op de grond ligt.
examples.english: I grab the ball that is on the ground.

examples.dutch: Vergeet niet je tas te pakken.
examples.english: Don't forget to grab your bag.

493.explanation: This phrase means to observe a football game, typically on television or live at a stadium. It's a common pastime.

examples.dutch: Wij gaan vanavond voetbal kijken met vrienden.
examples.english: We are going to watch football tonight with friends.

examples.dutch: Kijk jij graag voetbal in het weekend?
examples.english: Do you like watching football on the weekend?

494.explanation: To 'zoeken' means to try to find something or someone that is lost or hidden. You are actively looking for something.

examples.dutch: Ik zoek mijn sleutels, ik kan ze niet vinden.
examples.english: I am looking for my keys, I can't find them.

examples.dutch: Zij zoekt een nieuw boek om te lezen.
examples.english: She is looking for a new book to read.

495.explanation: This verb means to be startled or frightened by something sudden or unexpected. It often implies a sudden, momentary fear.

examples.dutch: Ik schrok van het harde geluid buiten.
examples.english: I was startled by the loud noise outside.

examples.dutch: Zij schrikken snel in het donker.
examples.english: They get scared easily in the dark.

496.explanation: This is a diminutive form of 'eten' (food/meal), referring to a small, informal dinner or a pleasant social meal. It often implies a cozy gathering.

examples.dutch: We hebben een gezellig etentje met de familie.
examples.english: We are having a nice dinner with the family.

examples.dutch: Het etentje in het restaurant was heerlijk.
examples.english: The dinner at the restaurant was delicious.

497.explanation: The 'koelkast' is a large kitchen appliance used to keep food and drinks cold and fresh. It is an essential part of most kitchens.

examples.dutch: Zet de melk terug in de koelkast, alsjeblieft.
examples.english: Put the milk back in the fridge, please.

examples.dutch: De koelkast is bijna leeg, we moeten boodschappen doen.
examples.english: The fridge is almost empty, we need to go grocery shopping.

498.explanation: A 'plank' is a flat, horizontal surface, typically made of wood or metal, used for storing things. It can be part of furniture or fixed to a wall.

examples.dutch: De boeken staan netjes op de plank.
examples.english: The books are neatly on the shelf.

examples.dutch: Zet het glas voorzichtig op de bovenste plank.
examples.english: Put the glass carefully on the top shelf.

499.explanation: The 'wijn' is an alcoholic drink made from fermented grapes, commonly served with meals or at social occasions.

examples.dutch: Ik drink graag een glas rode wijn bij het eten.
examples.english: I like to drink a glass of red wine with dinner.

examples.dutch: Kun je een fles witte wijn voor het feest kopen?
examples.english: Can you buy a bottle of white wine for the party?

500.explanation: A 'mes' is a tool with a sharp blade and a handle, primarily used for cutting food or other materials. Always handle it with care.

examples.dutch: Geef me het mes om het brood te snijden, alsjeblieft.
examples.english: Give me the knife to cut the bread, please.

examples.dutch: Pas op, het mes is erg scherp.
examples.english: Be careful, the knife is very sharp.

501.explanation: A 'plak' refers to a thin, flat piece cut from a larger object, such as a slice of bread, cheese, or meat.

examples.dutch: Ik wil graag een plak kaas op mijn brood.
examples.english: I would like a slice of cheese on my bread.

examples.dutch: Snijd een dunne plak van de komkommer.
examples.english: Cut a thin slice of the cucumber.

502.explanation: An 'oven' is a kitchen appliance with an enclosed compartment used for baking, roasting, or heating food. Many dishes are prepared in it.

examples.dutch: Het brood zit nu in de oven en is bijna klaar.
examples.english: The bread is now in the oven and is almost ready.

examples.dutch: Verwarm de oven voor op 180 graden Celsius.
examples.english: Preheat the oven to 180 degrees Celsius.

503.explanation: A 'pan' is a metal container used for cooking food, either on a stove or in an oven. There are many types of pans for different cooking methods.

examples.dutch: De soep kookt langzaam in de grote pan.
examples.english: The soup is slowly boiling in the big pot.

examples.dutch: Zet de pan met water op het vuur.
examples.english: Put the pan with water on the fire.

504.explanation: The 'vuur' refers to the light and heat produced when something burns. In a cooking context, it often means the flame or heat source on a stove.

examples.dutch: Pas op, het vuur onder de pan is heet.
examples.english: Be careful, the fire under the pan is hot.

examples.dutch: Zet het vuur lager als het water kookt.
examples.english: Turn down the heat/fire when the water boils.

505.explanation: A 'koekenpan' is a shallow, flat-bottomed pan, usually with a long handle, specifically designed for frying food. It's great for eggs or pancakes.

examples.dutch: Bak de eieren in de koekenpan met wat boter.
examples.english: Fry the eggs in the frying pan with some butter.

examples.dutch: Waar is de koekenpan? Ik wil pannenkoeken bakken.
examples.english: Where is the frying pan? I want to make pancakes.

506.explanation: A 'stuk' refers to a part of something that has been broken or separated from the whole. It can be a piece of food, a broken item, or a section of something.

examples.dutch: Ik wil graag een groot stuk taart, alsjeblieft.
examples.english: I would like a large piece of cake, please.

examples.dutch: Geef me een klein stuk fruit voor de lunch.
examples.english: Give me a small piece of fruit for lunch.

507.explanation: A 'biet' is a root vegetable with a distinct deep red or purple color, often eaten cooked, roasted, or pickled. It's known for its earthy taste.

examples.dutch: Ik eet graag gekookte biet bij mijn avondeten.
examples.english: I like to eat cooked beetroot with my dinner.

examples.dutch: De biet is een gezonde groente vol vitaminen.
examples.english: The beetroot is a healthy vegetable full of vitamins.

508.explanation: A liquid food, often hot, made by cooking ingredients like vegetables or meat in water or broth. It's a popular starter or light meal.

examples.dutch: Ik eet graag tomatensoep.
examples.english: I like to eat tomato soup.

examples.dutch: Is de soep warm?
examples.english: Is the soup warm?

509.explanation: A dairy product made from cream that has been fermented by bacteria, giving it a tangy, slightly sour taste and a thick texture. It's often used as a topping or in cooking.

examples.dutch: Ik doe zure room op mijn taco.
examples.english: I put sour cream on my taco.

examples.dutch: Heb je zure room nodig voor het recept?
examples.english: Do you need sour cream for the recipe?

510.explanation: A cucumber preserved in vinegar or brine, often used as a condiment or snack. It has a distinctive sour and sometimes sweet taste.

examples.dutch: Deze augurk is erg lekker.
examples.english: This pickle is very tasty.

examples.dutch: Ik wil een broodje met kaas en augurk.
examples.english: I want a sandwich with cheese and pickle.

511.explanation: A large, round citrus fruit with a slightly bitter, sour, or sweet taste, typically yellow or pink. It's often eaten for breakfast.

examples.dutch: Ik eet graag een halve grapefruit in de ochtend.
examples.english: I like to eat half a grapefruit in the morning.

examples.dutch: De grapefruit is roze en sappig.
examples.english: The grapefruit is pink and juicy.

512.explanation: A leafy vegetable with a slightly bitter taste, often used in salads or cooked. It has tightly packed, pale yellow-white leaves.

examples.dutch: Mijn moeder maakt een lekkere salade met witlof.
examples.english: My mother makes a tasty salad with chicory.

examples.dutch: Witlof is gezond.
examples.english: Chicory is healthy.

513.explanation: A flaky, buttery pastry, typically crescent-shaped, often eaten for breakfast. It originated in France but is popular worldwide.

examples.dutch: Ik koop een croissant bij de bakker.
examples.english: I buy a croissant at the bakery.

examples.dutch: Wil je thee met een croissant?
examples.english: Do you want tea with a croissant?

514.explanation: A sweet, sticky substance produced by bees, often used as a sweetener or spread. It comes from flower nectar.

examples.dutch: Ik doe honing in mijn thee.
examples.english: I put honey in my tea.

examples.dutch: De honing is van de bijen.
examples.english: The honey is from the bees.

515.explanation: Thin slices of potato that are deep-fried or baked until crispy and then seasoned, often eaten as a snack. In British English, this is 'crisps'.

examples.dutch: Ik eet graag chips op de bank.
examples.english: I like to eat chips on the couch.

examples.dutch: Welke smaak chips wil je?
examples.english: Which flavor of chips do you want?

516.explanation: A small, oily fish commonly found in the Atlantic and Pacific oceans. In the Netherlands, 'Hollandse Nieuwe' (new herring) is a popular traditional snack, often eaten raw with onions.

examples.dutch: Ik eet graag een Hollandse Nieuwe haring.
examples.english: I like to eat a new Dutch herring.

examples.dutch: Haring is een typisch Nederlandse delicatesse.
examples.english: Herring is a typical Dutch delicacy.

517.explanation: Small, dry fruits with a hard shell containing an edible kernel, like peanuts, almonds, or walnuts. They are often eaten as a snack. 'Nootjes' is the plural diminutive form, commonly used for snack nuts.

examples.dutch: Wij eten nootjes bij de borrel.
examples.english: We eat nuts with drinks.

examples.dutch: Ik koop een zakje nootjes.
examples.english: I buy a bag of nuts.

518.explanation: A person whom one knows and with whom one has a bond of mutual affection. This word typically refers to a male friend.

examples.dutch: Mijn vriend woont in Amsterdam.
examples.english: My friend lives in Amsterdam.

examples.dutch: Hij is een goede vriend van mij.
examples.english: He is a good friend of mine.

519.explanation: This phrase specifically refers to a romantic male partner. It indicates a boyfriend rather than just a platonic male friend.

examples.dutch: Mijn vriend komt vanavond eten.
examples.english: My boyfriend is coming to eat tonight.

examples.dutch: Ik ga met mijn vriend naar de film.
examples.english: I am going to the movie with my boyfriend.

520.explanation: A term of endearment used for someone you love, like a partner, child, or even a pet. It's like 'sweetheart' or 'honey'.

examples.dutch: Dag schatje, hoe gaat het?
examples.english: Hello darling, how are you?

examples.dutch: Je bent een echt schatje!
examples.english: You are a real darling!

521.explanation: A group of people related by blood, marriage, or adoption. This includes parents, children, siblings, and other relatives.

examples.dutch: Mijn familie is erg belangrijk voor mij.
examples.english: My family is very important to me.

examples.dutch: Wij gaan met de hele familie uit eten.
examples.english: We are going out to eat with the whole family.

522.explanation: A social gathering of people for entertainment, celebration, or enjoyment. The diminutive form 'feestje' often implies a smaller, more informal party.

examples.dutch: Ik ga naar een verjaardagsfeestje.
examples.english: I am going to a birthday party.

examples.dutch: Het feestje begint om acht uur.
examples.english: The party starts at eight o'clock.

523.explanation: This phrase means 'a little bit' or 'a short distance' when referring to physical space or time. It implies something is not very far or long.

examples.dutch: Het is nog een eindje lopen.
examples.english: It's still a small distance to walk.

examples.dutch: Ik woon hier een eindje vandaan.
examples.english: I live a small distance from here.

524.explanation: The diminutive form of 'jongen' (boy), specifically referring to a young male child. It emphasizes that he is small or young.

examples.dutch: Het jongetje speelt buiten met de bal.
examples.english: The little boy is playing outside with the ball.

examples.dutch: Waar is het jongetje met de rode muts?
examples.english: Where is the little boy with the red hat?

525.explanation: An activity played for enjoyment or competition, following certain rules. This can refer to board games, card games, or sports games.

examples.dutch: We spelen een leuk spel vanavond.
examples.english: We are playing a fun game tonight.

examples.dutch: Houd je van dit spel?
examples.english: Do you like this game?

526.explanation: This word can mean a female friend or, when used with a possessive pronoun like 'mijn,' it specifically refers to a romantic female partner (girlfriend).

examples.dutch: Zij is mijn beste vriendin.
examples.english: She is my best friend.

examples.dutch: Mijn vriendin komt uit Spanje.
examples.english: My girlfriend is from Spain.

527.explanation: The point or area where two lines or surfaces meet. This can refer to a street corner, a room corner, or the corner of an object.

examples.dutch: Het huis staat op de hoek van de straat.
examples.english: The house is on the corner of the street.

examples.dutch: De stoel staat in de hoek van de kamer.
examples.english: The chair is in the corner of the room.

528.explanation: De kroeg is a Dutch word for a pub or a bar, a place where people go to drink and socialize. It's often a cozy, informal spot.

examples.dutch: Ik ga vanavond naar de kroeg.
examples.english: I am going to the pub tonight.

examples.dutch: Deze kroeg heeft lekker bier.
examples.english: This pub has tasty beer.

529.explanation: Het biertje refers to a small glass or serving of beer. It often implies a single, specific drink.

examples.dutch: Mag ik een biertje, alstublieft?
examples.english: May I have a beer, please?

examples.dutch: Hij drinkt graag een koud biertje.
examples.english: He likes to drink a cold beer.

530.explanation: De woning is a formal word for a dwelling or a residence, meaning a place where people live. It can be a house or an apartment.

examples.dutch: Deze woning is te koop.
examples.english: This residence is for sale.

examples.dutch: Ze wonen in een mooie woning.
examples.english: They live in a beautiful house.

531.explanation: De pauze is a short break from work, study, or any activity. It's a time to rest or do something else briefly.

examples.dutch: We hebben pauze om twaalf uur.
examples.english: We have a break at twelve o'clock.

examples.dutch: Tijdens de pauze drink ik koffie.
examples.english: During the break, I drink coffee.

532.explanation: Het papier is the material used for writing, printing, or drawing. It can also refer to a specific document or sheet.

examples.dutch: Ik schrijf op een stuk papier.
examples.english: I write on a piece of paper.

examples.dutch: Het papier is wit en schoon.
examples.english: The paper is white and clean.

533.explanation: De bril is a pair of glasses, used to help someone see better or to protect their eyes. You wear it on your face.

examples.dutch: Waar is mijn bril?
examples.english: Where are my glasses?

examples.dutch: Ze draagt een mooie bril.
examples.english: She wears nice glasses.

534.explanation: Een kwart means 'a quarter', which is one-fourth of something. It can refer to a part of an object, time, or quantity.

examples.dutch: Het is kwart over drie.
examples.english: It is a quarter past three.

examples.dutch: Ik eet een kwart van de pizza.
examples.english: I eat a quarter of the pizza.

535.explanation: Het zeeniveau refers to the average height of the sea's surface. It is often used as a reference point for measuring elevation on land.

examples.dutch: Een deel van Nederland ligt onder zeeniveau.
examples.english: A part of the Netherlands is below sea level.

examples.dutch: De berg is hoog boven zeeniveau.
examples.english: The mountain is high above sea level.

536.explanation: De Nederlander is a person who comes from the Netherlands. It refers to a Dutch citizen or resident.

examples.dutch: Ik ben een Nederlander.
examples.english: I am a Dutch person.

examples.dutch: Veel Nederlanders fietsen graag.
examples.english: Many Dutch people like to cycle.

537.explanation: De helft means 'the half', which is one of two equal parts of something. You often use it when dividing things.

examples.dutch: Ik eet de helft van de appel.
examples.english: I eat half of the apple.

examples.dutch: Geef mij de helft van het geld.
examples.english: Give me half of the money.

538.explanation: Een vijfde means 'one fifth', which is one out of five equal parts of a whole. It's a way to express a fraction.

examples.dutch: Een vijfde deel van de groep is afwezig.
examples.english: One fifth of the group is absent.

examples.dutch: We verdelen de taart in vijfde delen.
examples.english: We divide the cake into fifths.

539.explanation: Het water is the common liquid that humans and animals need to live. It's essential for drinking, washing, and many other things.

examples.dutch: Ik drink graag koud water.
examples.english: I like to drink cold water.

examples.dutch: Het water in de rivier is schoon.
examples.english: The water in the river is clean.

540.explanation: Driekwart means 'three quarters', which is three out of four equal parts of something. It describes a large portion.

examples.dutch: De fles is driekwart leeg.
examples.english: The bottle is three quarters empty.

examples.dutch: Driekwart van de studenten komt op tijd.
examples.english: Three quarters of the students arrive on time.

541.explanation: De bloembol is a round, underground part of a plant that stores food and helps a flower grow. Think of tulip bulbs.

examples.dutch: Ik plant bloembollen in de tuin.
examples.english: I plant flower bulbs in the garden.

examples.dutch: Deze bloembol wordt een mooie tulp.
examples.english: This flower bulb will become a beautiful tulip.

542.explanation: De wereld refers to the Earth, the planet where we live. It can also mean all the people or things on Earth.

examples.dutch: De wereld is groot en mooi.
examples.english: The world is big and beautiful.

examples.dutch: Veel mensen reizen de wereld rond.
examples.english: Many people travel around the world.

543.explanation: De bal is a round object used in many sports and games. You can throw, kick, or bounce it.

examples.dutch: Ik speel met een grote bal.
examples.english: I play with a big ball.

examples.dutch: De hond rent achter de bal aan.
examples.english: The dog runs after the ball.

544.explanation: De bus is a large vehicle that carries many passengers along a fixed route. It's a common form of public transport.

examples.dutch: Ik neem de bus naar mijn werk.
examples.english: I take the bus to my work.

examples.dutch: De bushalte is dichtbij.
examples.english: The bus stop is nearby.

545.explanation: Het podium is a raised platform where performers, speakers, or musicians stand during a show or event. It makes them visible to the audience.

examples.dutch: De zanger staat op het podium.
examples.english: The singer stands on the stage.

examples.dutch: Het publiek kijkt naar het podium.
examples.english: The audience looks at the stage.

546.explanation: Het orkest is a large group of musicians who play various instruments together, often performing classical music. They usually play on a stage.

examples.dutch: Het orkest speelt prachtige muziek.
examples.english: The orchestra plays beautiful music.

examples.dutch: Mijn broer speelt in een orkest.
examples.english: My brother plays in an orchestra.

547.explanation: Het paard is a large, strong animal with four legs, often used for riding or pulling carts. They are known for their speed and grace.

examples.dutch: Ik rijd graag op een paard.
examples.english: I like to ride a horse.

examples.dutch: Het paard eet gras in de wei.
examples.english: The horse eats grass in the meadow.

548.explanation: This refers to an open field, often green, where animals like cows or sheep graze. It's a common sight in rural areas.

examples.dutch: De koeien staan in de wei.
examples.english: The cows are in the pasture.

examples.dutch: Ik zie groene wei.
examples.english: I see green grassland.

549.explanation: This is a set of rooms on one floor, forming a private residence within a larger building. Many people live in an apartment in the city.

examples.dutch: Ik woon in een appartement.
examples.english: I live in an apartment.

examples.dutch: Het appartement is groot.
examples.english: The apartment is big.

550.explanation: This word means a level of a building, like the first or second floor. It can also refer to the surface you walk on inside a room.

examples.dutch: Mijn kantoor is op de eerste verdieping.
examples.english: My office is on the first floor.

examples.dutch: Het appartement heeft drie verdiepingen.
examples.english: The apartment has three floors.

551.explanation: This refers to an instance or occasion, not time in general (which is 'de tijd'). You use it when you talk about 'one time' or 'many times'.

examples.dutch: Dit is de eerste keer.
examples.english: This is the first time.

examples.dutch: Eén keer per week.
examples.english: Once a week.

552.explanation: This is a common toast used when drinking with others, similar to 'cheers' in English. You say it before you take a sip.

examples.dutch: We drinken wijn, proost!
examples.english: We are drinking wine, cheers!

examples.dutch: Proost op je gezondheid!
examples.english: Cheers to your health!

553.explanation: This is the Dutch name for the country Italy, famous for its food, history, and art. It's located in Southern Europe.

examples.dutch: Ik ga op vakantie naar Italië.
examples.english: I am going on holiday to Italy.

examples.dutch: Pizza komt uit Italië.
examples.english: Pizza comes from Italy.
